arts.ttl: Middlesbrough 'trust' in Charlton Athletic and thinking behind Alex Gilbert's loan move
arts.sum: A straight loan with no purchase option, Boro still see a big future for the attacking midfielder and are sending him to Charlton in the hope he continues his development by playing regularly in the second half of the season. Alex Gilbert completed a deadline day Middlesbrough exit as he joined League One side Charlton Athletic on loan until the end of the season.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough medical on Monday for player – Deal with Carrick’s side ‘good operation’ for club
arts.sum: Sevilla’s Kelechi Iheanacho is on his way to Middlesbrough on deadline day of the winter market, according to claims from Spain. The striker has struggled for prominence under manager García Pimienta and the La Liga club have decided to let him leave. On Friday, Diario de Sevilla reported the 28-year-old has attracted interest from France, The post Middlesbrough medical on Monday for player – Deal with Carrick’s side ‘good operation’ for club appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: What to expect on transfer deadline day as Middlesbrough plan for busy final day
arts.sum: Middlesbrough will complete loan deals on transfer deadline day that will see Kelechi Iheanacho and Samuel Iling-Junior join to bolster their attacking ranks. As far as more concrete outgoings are concerned, Emmanuel Latte Lath is on the verge of a move to Atlanta United and Lukas Engel is still expected to join FC Cincinnati on a loan including an option to buy.

arts.ttl: David Strelec summer move revelation likely explains Middlesbrough's Kelechi Iheanacho move
arts.sum: A Slovan Bratislava director says that striker David Strelec is far more likely to leave the club in the summer transfer window - as he commented on interest in the 23-year-old from Middlesbrough and Celtic. Commenting on interest this month in Strelec, meanwhile, Slovan Bratislava director general Ivan KmotrIk told Sport : "If David leaves, it will definitely be a Slovak record.

arts.ttl: Busy Middlesbrough transfer window 'shows intent' as Michael Carrick makes top-two admission
arts.sum: With games taking place over the weekend ahead of Boro’s clash with Sunderland on Monday evening, Carrick’s side dropped to seventh in the league table, but can leapfrog Blackburn and West Brom back up to fifth, providing they claim all three points against their North East rivals. Asked if that showed Boro’s intent this season, Carrick said: “It shows we’re pushing and want to keep improving.
